date_original: last third of Tishrei – first third of Elul 1452
description:
Folio from a court notebook. Location: Minyat Zifta. There are 3 betrothal (erusin/qiddushin) documents (all edited in Ashur's dissertation) and a 4th document concerning a debt (unedited). 

Document #1: Dated: last third of Tishrei 1452 Seleucid (= October 1140 CE), under the authority of the Nagid Shemuʾel b. Ḥananya. This is the earliest attestation of Shemuʾel as Nagid. Fiancé: Natan(?) b. Avraham ha-Kohen. Fiancée: [...] bt. Yosef ha-Kohen. Early marriage payment: 10 dinars. Delayed marriage payment: 40 dinars. Witnessed by Shabbetay b. Avraham (active 1135–78) and Yeshuʿa b. Shemuel. 

Document #2: The fiancé Yefet b. Yaʿaqov accepts the condition that he will not make his future wife move away from Minyat Zifta. Witnessed by Yeshuʿa b. Shemuel. 

Document #3: The fiancé is named Yefet. The document is too faded to discern many of the details. Mentions "laʿb" (playing musical instruments? games? gambling?). Dated first third of Shevaṭ 1452 Seleucid. 

Document #4: Concerns the remaining money owed to Tammām b. Nissīm by a certain Abū l-Faraj. Dated: first third of Elul 1452.
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית מהמאה ה-12. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
